ogTitle: West Nowra vs West Pennant Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Nowra and West Pennant Hills.
heading: West Nowra vs West Pennant Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Nowra, New South Wales and West Pennant Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Pennant Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to West Nowra (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Nowra or West Pennant Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Nowra has a flood risk score of 49/100, while West Pennant Hills scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Nowra scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. West Pennant Hills scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Nowra or West Pennant Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Pennant Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.42% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
